summary: Wired LeaveGroupButton to a three-way modal (abandon/blocked/confirm) driven by selectLeaveModalState, reading live MLS members on modal open via a new MarmotContext.getLiveMemberPubkeys method. Added en/de abandon copy and moved the dead leave-group-modal testid off <Modal> onto <ModalContent>.
design_decisions: handleOpen() awaits getLiveMemberPubkeys before calling onOpen() (not in parallel / not fire-and-forget), and both setLiveMembers + onOpen happen synchronously after the await resolves — React 18 automatic batching means the modal never renders with a stale liveMembers value from a prior open.

modalState is derived in render (const modalState = selectLeaveModalState(...)) rather than stored in state, so it always reflects the latest liveMembers/adminPubkeys/ownPubkeyHex without a separate effect.

The 'abandon' modal's cancel button carries isDisabled={isLoading} matching the existing normal-leave modal's cancel button (not BlockContactButton's ModalCloseButton-disables pattern) — per exploration.json's modal_precedent.decided_default, the ModalCloseButton itself is NOT disabled in either modal.
isLoading is the single pre-existing component-level state variable, shared across the abandon and normal-leave confirm buttons — no new loading-state variable was introduced (AC-UX-3).

verification_answers.answer: git diff --stat confirms exactly 3 files touched: app/src/components/groups/LeaveGroupButton.tsx, app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx, app/src/lib/i18n.ts. The scoped MarmotContext.tsx diff shows only getLiveMemberPubkeys added (interface line, implementation block, two occurrences in the value/useMemo-deps list, one DEFAULT_MARMOT no-op) — leaveGroup, grantAdmin, and every other existing method are byte-for-byte unchanged in the diff.

getLiveMemberPubkeys lives entirely inside MarmotContext.tsx: it wraps clientRef.current.groups.get(groupId) (mirroring the null-safe getGroup pattern at :1655) plus a dynamic await import('@internet-privacy/marmot-ts') for getGroupMembers, matching the context's other dynamic-import sites. grep -c "@internet-privacy/marmot-ts" app/src/components/groups/LeaveGroupButton.tsx returns 0 — the component contains no import, static or dynamic, referencing the package, and calls only useMarmot().getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId).

LeaveGroupButtonProps is unchanged except in spirit: groupId, adminPubkeys?, ownPubkeyHex? — no memberPubkeys prop was added before or after this diff. The member list arrives exclusively via getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId) called in handleOpen(), stored in local liveMembers state; groups.tsx's call site (which already passes only groupId/adminPubkeys/ownPubkeyHex per exploration.json) was not touched and needs no change.

abandon-group-notice sits on a <Text> element inside <ModalBody>; abandon-group-confirm-btn sits on a <Button> inside <ModalFooter> — both real DOM-producing elements. The pre-existing dead data-testid="leave-group-modal" was moved from <Modal> (line 91 pre-diff) onto <ModalContent> (line 139 post-diff) in the 'confirm' branch, mirroring BlockContactButton.tsx:110. It was not left in place and not silently deleted.

No second loading-state variable was introduced. The single pre-existing const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false) is used by handleLeave() (set true at start, false in finally) and is bound identically to both the abandon-group-confirm-btn and the pre-existing leave-group-confirm-btn's isLoading prop.

The render is an if/else-if/else chain over modalState === 'abandon' | 'blocked' | (else, i.e. 'confirm'). Since selectLeaveModalState's return type is the literal union 'abandon' | 'blocked' | 'confirm', the else branch is exhaustively 'confirm' — there is no default/fallthrough case and no state value renders nothing.

liveMembers is local useState<string[] | undefined>(undefined), set only inside handleOpen() from the resolved (or fail-closed undefined) result of getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId) — read fresh on every modal open, never a prop, never a value captured at an earlier render and reused. When the read throws, the catch sets members = undefined before setLiveMembers(members), so undefined (never [] or a stale array) is what selectLeaveModalState receives. No memberPubkeys prop exists on the component (cross-checked with VQ-S2-003).

abandon-group-confirm-btn's isLoading prop is bound to the exact same isLoading variable as leave-group-confirm-btn (cross-check VQ-S2-005) — both buttons call the same handleLeave(), which sets/clears isLoading identically regardless of which modal state triggered it. No new double-activation hazard beyond what the existing normal-leave confirm already tolerates: Chakra's isLoading disables pointer interaction on the button while true, same as today.

getLiveMemberPubkeys has three return points: (1) !client -> return undefined; (2) mlsGroup falsy after client.groups.get(groupId).catch(() => null) -> return undefined; (3) the catch block wrapping the dynamic import + getGroupMembers call -> return undefined. There is no code path that returns []. A legitimate 'group exists but has zero members' case is not reachable in this codebase — every MLS group has at least the caller as a member by construction (creation adds the creator; solo-abandon is the minimum-membership state at exactly 1, never 0) — so the only way to get an empty-looking result is the unreadable path, which correctly returns undefined, not [].

grep -n "abandonGroupTitle\|abandonGroupBody\|abandonGroupConfirm" app/src/lib/i18n.ts returns exactly 9 matches: 3 in the Copy type (lines 129-131) + 3 in en (lines 755-757) + 3 in de (lines 1292-1294). All six string values are non-empty, non-placeholder prose (verified by direct read, not grep alone).

grep -c "@internet-privacy/marmot-ts" app/src/components/groups/LeaveGroupButton.tsx returns 0, both before and after this story's diff (the component never imported marmot-ts). The diff adding getLiveMemberPubkeys-consuming code (handleOpen, the useMarmot() destructure) introduces zero import/await-import lines referencing @internet-privacy/marmot-ts. The likely-wrong static-import mistake was not made — the read is entirely delegated to context.getLiveMemberPubkeys(groupId).

BEFORE (git show HEAD, i.e. pre-story tree): grep -nE '<Modal\b' | grep -vE '<ModalContent|<ModalOverlay' matches two lines, line 102 of which is `<Modal isOpen={isOpen} onClose={onClose} isCentered data-testid="leave-group-modal">` — the check FAILS (a <Modal> element does carry a data-testid). AFTER (working tree): the same scan matches three <Modal\b> lines (one per branch, now 3 branches instead of 2), and grep -c "data-testid" over those lines returns 0 — the check now PASSES. The dead testid now lives on <ModalContent> in the 'confirm' branch instead.

testing.unit: make test-unit: 172 test files, 3338 passed, 1 skipped (pre-existing, unrelated) — no regressions.
testing.e2e: Not run — out of scope for S2 per the story brief (S4 owns AC-FLOW-1/2/3).

retrospective.reason: clean run, no friction worth recording
